data.plantuml:
@startuml
title Metro platform screen door control upgrade
left to right direction
component "Central PSD controller" as PSDC
component "Door control units" as DCU
component "Door position sensors" as POS
component "Obstacle detection" as OBS
component "Train berth detection" as BERTH
component "Signalling interface" as SIG
component "Local control panel" as LCP
component "Operations control centre" as OCC
component "Maintenance terminal" as MAINT
component "Event recorder" as LOG
component "Backed-up power" as UPS
component "Mechanical platform doors" as DOOR

BERTH --> PSDC : train aligned
SIG --> PSDC : enable and departure interface
POS --> DCU : closed and locked state
OBS --> DCU : obstruction state
PSDC --> DCU : open and close commands
DCU --> DOOR : door actuation
DCU --> PSDC : door status
LCP --> PSDC : authorized local control
PSDC --> OCC : alarms and availability
MAINT --> PSDC : diagnostics and configuration
PSDC --> LOG : events
UPS --> PSDC : backed-up supply
UPS --> DCU : backed-up supply
@enduml

situations.name: Normal passenger service
situations.description: A correctly berthed train exchanges passengers while train and platform doors operate in coordination.

situations.name: Train misalignment
situations.description: A train stops outside the permitted berth tolerance and normal door enablement is unavailable.

situations.name: Door obstruction
situations.description: A passenger or object obstructs a platform door during closing.

situations.name: Single door isolated
situations.description: One platform door leaf is secured out of service while restricted passenger service continues.

situations.name: Local manual control
situations.description: Authorized station staff use the local control panel following an automatic-control failure.

situations.name: Maintenance with possession
situations.description: Technicians isolate, diagnose, replace, and test PSD equipment during protected engineering hours.

situations.name: Power loss and restoration
situations.description: Normal supply is lost, backed-up power operates, and supplies are subsequently restored.

situations.name: Staged cutover
situations.description: New and existing control equipment coexist during installation and commissioning.

hazards.name: Train movement with platform opening unsafe
hazards.description: Departure is permitted while a platform opening is not closed, locked, isolated, or otherwise controlled.

hazards.name: Passenger trapped by closing door
hazards.description: A passenger or object is trapped or dragged by closing platform or train doors.

hazards.name: Door opens without correctly berthed train
hazards.description: A platform door opens when no train doorway safely covers the platform opening.

hazards.name: Unsafe degraded operation
hazards.description: A bypass, isolation, or local-control process leaves an opening or departure condition uncontrolled.

hazards.name: Unexpected movement during maintenance
hazards.description: A door moves or stored energy is released while a technician is exposed.

hazards.name: Loss of evacuation route
hazards.description: Door state or control failure obstructs passenger evacuation or responder access.

hazards.name: Electrical exposure
hazards.description: Personnel contact hazardous energy during installation, fault finding, or replacement.

hazards.name: Crowding caused by prolonged PSD unavailability
hazards.description: Service disruption and platform crowding introduce secondary passenger risks.

requirements.text: The PSD system shall achieve at least 99.95% operational availability during scheduled traffic hours, using the approved failure and downtime counting rules.
requirements.verification: Operational reliability demonstration and monthly performance report

requirements.text: A failed door control unit shall be diagnosable, replaceable, configured, and functionally restored within 45 minutes after safe maintenance access is granted.
requirements.verification: Timed maintainability demonstration with trained maintainer and production tools

requirements.text: The signalling interface shall not provide a valid platform-secure condition unless every required door is proved closed and locked or is covered by an authorized degraded-mode configuration.
requirements.verification: End-to-end departure-permission test including open, unlocked, isolated, stale-data, and communications-fault cases

requirements.text: The PSD controller shall enable automatic opening only when valid train-berth information confirms alignment within the approved tolerance.
Each door control unit shall detect an obstruction during closing and command the validated reopen or stop response within the specified detection and response time.
requirements.verification: Obstacle detection tests using approved test pieces and approach conditions

requirements.text: Local control and maintenance bypasses shall require authorization, be visibly indicated locally and remotely, be event logged, and expire or be removed through a controlled process.
requirements.verification: Role, indication, logging, timeout, and restoration tests

requirements.text: Following power restoration, automatic door operation and platform-secure status shall remain inhibited until required inputs, configuration, and door states are valid.
Maintenance replacement of a door control unit shall require a post-installation functional test and configuration check before return to passenger service.
requirements.verification: Observed maintenance demonstration and controlled work instruction review

silAssessments.safetyFunction: Prevent departure with an unsafe platform opening
silAssessments.hazardousEvent: A train departs while a platform opening is not safely controlled.
silAssessments.safeState: Departure permission withheld and affected door state alarmed
silAssessments.evidence: Training discussion only; formal integrity allocation requires the accepted EN 50126-2 method

quantitative.safetyFunction: Prevent departure with an unsafe platform opening

notepad.html:
RAMS assumptions workshop

Separate confirmed facts from assumptions before accepting RAMS targets or safety claims.

AssumptionRAMS impactOwnerEvidence needed
Existing mechanical doors have sufficient remaining lifeReliability and safetyAsset engineeringCondition survey and duty-cycle history
Maintenance access is granted within 20 minutesMaintainability and availabilityOperationsPossession and response-time data
Train berth data meets the required integrity and timingSafety and availabilitySignallingInterface specification and validation evidence

Review the Architecture, Engineering workflow, and Safety requirements.

brainstormRows.failureMode: Train berth status absent or stale while automatic opening is requested
brainstormRows.effect: Doors may be inhibited unnecessarily or enabled without confirmed train alignment
brainstormRows.action: Add freshness timeout, plausibility checks, fail-safe invalid state, and end-to-end interface validation

phases.name: 1 Concept
phases.purpose: Establish the project purpose, RAMS policy, feasibility, stakeholders, and lifecycle strategy.

phases.name: 2 System definition
phases.purpose: Define the system, operational context, interfaces, assumptions, and application constraints.

phases.name: 3 Risk analysis
phases.purpose: Identify hazards, analyze risk, evaluate acceptability, and establish hazard ownership.

phases.name: 4 Requirements
phases.purpose: Specify measurable system RAMS and safety requirements.

phases.name: 5 Architecture
phases.purpose: Develop architecture and apportion RAMS requirements across subsystems and interfaces.

phases.name: 6 Design
phases.purpose: Implement the design under verification, configuration, quality, and safety controls.

phases.name: 7 Manufacture
phases.purpose: Control production, inspection, conformity, and manufacturing evidence.

phases.name: 8 Integration
phases.purpose: Integrate subsystems and interfaces while controlling staged-installation risk.

phases.name: 9 Validation
phases.purpose: Validate integrated RAMS performance in representative operational contexts.

phases.name: 10 Acceptance
phases.purpose: Assemble the safety demonstration and support authorized system acceptance.

phases.name: 11 Operation
phases.purpose: Operate, maintain, monitor performance, investigate trends, and control modifications.

phases.name: 12 Decommissioning
phases.purpose: Remove or replace assets without introducing uncontrolled risk or losing required records.

activities.title: Establish RAMS management and lifecycle tailoring
activities.objective: Define RAMS policy, plan, roles, competence, independence, deliverables, configuration, and tailoring.
activities.inputs: Business case, standards baseline, existing asset strategy
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are omitted or reused activities justified with compensating controls and authorized approval?
activities.completionCriteria: RAMS plan, tailoring, roles, interfaces, and independent-review strategy are approved.
activities.evidence: Draft RAMS plan RP-01; tailoring workshop open

activities.title: Define system and operational context
activities.objective: Define the System under Consideration, boundary, functions, interfaces, actors, environment, and assumptions.
activities.inputs: Operating concept, surveys, interface data, legacy records
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Could an omitted interface, degraded mode, human role, or legacy constraint invalidate RAMS analysis?
activities.completionCriteria: System definition, contexts, interfaces, and assumptions are reviewed by all affected organizations.

activities.title: Perform risk analysis and establish hazard log
activities.objective: Identify hazards, causes, consequences, controls, acceptance principles, owners, and residual risk.
activities.inputs: System definition, operating scenarios, incident history
activities.outputs: Controlled hazard log and risk evaluation
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are normal, degraded, maintenance, integration, emergency, and human-factor scenarios represented?
activities.standardReference: EN 50126-2 risk assessment and hazard control
activities.completionCriteria: Hazards have owners, accepted evaluation methods, controls, and traceable actions.
activities.evidence: Initial hazard workshop completed; acceptance method pending

activities.title: Specify system RAMS requirements
activities.objective: Define measurable reliability, availability, maintainability, and safety requirements with acceptance rules.
activities.inputs: Stakeholder needs, hazard log, service plan, maintenance concept
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are measures, boundaries, counting rules, operating conditions, and evidence methods explicit?
activities.completionCriteria: RAMS requirements are measurable, traceable, reviewed, and suitable for validation.

activities.title: Develop architecture and apportion requirements
activities.objective: Allocate end-to-end RAMS requirements to subsystems, interfaces, procedures, and organizations.
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are interface failures, common causes, degraded modes, and human controls included in allocation?
activities.completionCriteria: Allocated requirements preserve the system-level claim and have identified verification methods.

activities.title: Design and verify the solution
activities.objective: Develop hardware, software, interfaces, diagnostics, maintenance features, and procedures under control.
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Does the design address systematic faults, random failures, human factors, maintainability, and cybersecurity impacts?
activities.standardReference: EN 50126-1 phase 6 and applicable domain standards
activities.completionCriteria: Design outputs are verified against allocated requirements with controlled evidence.

activities.title: Control manufacture and installation preparation
activities.objective: Ensure manufactured equipment conforms to released design and remains traceable.
activities.inputs: Released design, production plan, inspection criteria
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Can manufacturing or configuration variation invalidate RAMS evidence?
activities.completionCriteria: Equipment identity, inspection, nonconformance, and release evidence are complete.

activities.title: Integrate and commission staged interfaces
activities.objective: Integrate equipment, legacy assets, signalling, train interfaces, operations, and maintenance tools.
activities.inputs: Released equipment, interface specifications, cutover plan
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are temporary states, mixed configurations, rollback, isolation, and interface timing controlled?
activities.completionCriteria: Integration tests and configuration records show controlled interfaces and resolved anomalies.

activities.title: Validate integrated RAMS performance
activities.objective: Demonstrate the system satisfies RAMS requirements in representative operational conditions.
activities.inputs: Validated requirements, integrated system, operational scenarios
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Do tests cover faults, degraded operation, maintenance, environmental conditions, and application assumptions?
activities.completionCriteria: Validation evidence meets acceptance criteria and unresolved deviations are formally controlled.

activities.title: Complete safety demonstration and acceptance
activities.objective: Assemble arguments and evidence, complete independent review, and support authorized acceptance.
activities.inputs: Safety case, validation report, assessment findings
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are claims configuration-specific, evidence complete, assumptions valid, and restrictions transferred to operations?
activities.standardReference: EN 50126-1 phase 10 and safety case; EN 50126-2 safety acceptance
activities.completionCriteria: Assessment findings are closed or accepted and authorized acceptance conditions are recorded.

activities.title: Operate, maintain, and monitor RAMS performance
activities.objective: Collect performance data, investigate failures, maintain competence, and control modifications.
activities.inputs: Accepted system, maintenance plan, RAMS targets
activities.outputs: Performance reports, corrective actions, updated evidence
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Could trends, repeat faults, bypasses, or organizational changes invalidate RAMS assumptions?
activities.completionCriteria: Indicators, trigger levels, ownership, investigation, and change-control processes are operational.

activities.title: Plan safe decommissioning
activities.objective: Control removal, temporary arrangements, records, interfaces, disposal, and residual responsibilities.
activities.inputs: Replacement strategy, asset records, operational constraints
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Can partial removal, temporary wiring, lost data, or transferred responsibility introduce new risk?
activities.completionCriteria: Decommissioning hazards, controls, records, and responsibilities are reviewed and approved.

fmea.effect: Platform-secure status may be asserted with an open or unlocked door
fmea.action: Use monitored diverse state evidence, discrepancy detection, and end-to-end proof testing

fmea.failureMode: Door motor output remains energized after close command
fmea.effect: Excess closing force or continued movement can trap a passenger
fmea.action: Add current, position, and timeout monitoring with removal of drive energy

fmea.effect: Closing response may not detect a trapped passenger or object
fmea.action: Validate diagnostic coverage, detection geometry, contamination response, and periodic proof test

fmea.failureMode: Maintenance bypass remains active after work
fmea.effect: Automatic protection or indication can remain defeated during passenger service
fmea.action: Use authorization, prominent indication, event logging, timeout, and mandatory restoration test

fmea.failureMode: Backed-up supply unavailable on loss of normal power
fmea.effect: Door state, indication, or emergency operation may be unavailable
fmea.action: Monitor battery condition, load-test periodically, and define safe low-energy behavior
